programme.title: NHK World English News Morning
programme.desc: English news from Japan's Broadcasting Corporation, NHK, delivering the latest in business, technology, health, environmental and world news.

programme.title: ANC Philippines The World Tonight
programme.desc: Delivers the top stories of the day, covering political, economic, and social sectors on both local and global fronts.

programme.title: DW English News
programme.desc: News and analysis of the top international and European news and current affairs from Berlin, in English.

programme.title: Al Jazeera News
programme.desc: News via satellite from Al Jazeera Satellite Network, Qatar, in English.

programme.title: BBC 6 O'Clock News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news stories from the BBC News team from London, UK, in English.

programme.title: France 24 English News
programme.desc: International news and current affairs television from Paris, offering a French perspective on world events, in English.

programme.title: DD India News Hour
programme.desc: The most recent political, sports, entertainment and socio-economic updates across India and the world from New Delhi, in English.

programme.title: The Cook Up with Adam Liaw Bitesize
programme.sub-title: Almond Pudding With Ginger And Palm Sugar Syrup
programme.desc: Join host Adam Liaw and all your favourite chefs, cooks and celebrities for the best bits from The Cook Up kitchen.

programme.title: The Cook Up with Adam Liaw Bitesize
programme.desc: Join host Adam Liaw and all your favourite chefs, cooks and celebrities for the best bits from The Cook Up kitchen.

programme.title: Matched
programme.sub-title: The Muslim Wedding Of Rehana And Jansher / Ravinder And Manpreet's Union Of Two Souls
programme.desc: Muslim couple Rehana and Jansher have booked a date, but it could all come grinding to a halt if Jansher's mother doesn't give her approval when she meets the bride for the first time just days out from the wedding. And Indian bride Manpreet faces a wedding in New Zealand without the involvement of her loving parents.

programme.title: Kew Gardens: Season by Season
programme.desc: Summer means roses in Kew Gardens and the visitors are flocking to the historic Rose Garden to take them in, whilst Dave tackles the massive dead heading job that they bring. Meanwhile Kevin, Head of Tree Collections, is contemplating another epic pruning job.

programme.title: DNA Journey
programme.desc: Actors and friends, James Nesbitt and Sarah Parish, team up to delve into their family histories. A journey that starts in Northern Ireland where they met to discover the huge part education played in their lives both having teacher parents. To Sarah's surprise, her journey then takes them over to Scotland to look at her famous ancestor who changed schooling in Scotland forever.

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: The latest national and international news stories from the BBC News team from London, UK, in English.

programme.title: France 24 International News
programme.desc: International news and current affairs television from Paris, offering a French perspective on world events, in English.

programme.title: PBS NewsHour
programme.desc: Reviews and analysis of the day's news presented by Amna Nawaz and Geoff Bennett for the Public Broadcasting Service (PBS) in the United States.

programme.title: Rhoda Roberts AO: A Lasting Legacy
programme.sub-title: Forever Held In Country: Honouring Widjabul Wiyebal Woman Rhoda Roberts Ao
programme.desc: Paying homage to Bundjalung Widjabul Wiebal woman Rhoda Roberts AO this program honours her deep connection to Country and her legacy.

programme.title: NITV News: Nula
programme.desc: The latest news from the oldest living culture, join Natalie Ahmat and the team of journalists for stories from an Indigenous perspective.

programme.title: The Cook Up with Adam Liaw
programme.desc: Tonight's food is quick and easy, as Adam and his guests, musician Elly-May Barnes and chef Hamish Ingham make fast fresh bites.

programme.title: The Mayfair Hotel Megabuild
programme.desc: With the shell nearing completion, world-class designers add the finishing touches. Parisian interior designer Remi Tessier takes on the penthouse, where only the rarest materials will suffice for a rumoured 100,000 pound-a-night price tag. In the basement, Inge Theron combines Japanese scents with cutting-edge skincare technology to create Claridge's first spa.

programme.title: Jeopardy!
programme.desc: Ken Jennings hosts Jeopardy! Where the answers are revealed, and the contestants must guess the appropriate questions.

programme.title: Letters and Numbers
programme.desc: Two contestants pit their linguistic and numerical skills against each other and the clock in this entertaining quiz show. Join host Richard Morecroft along with maths whiz Lily Serna and wordsmith David Astle.

programme.title: Mastermind Australia
programme.desc: Our weekly family winners are back with two minutes of general knowledge, plus the dreaded slow burn round. Tonight's slow burn topics are a TV Show, a Food, a Landmark and a Song.

programme.title: SBS World News
programme.desc: The latest reports and analysis of major national and international news of the day from Australia's only world news service.

programme.title: Going Places With Ernie Dingo
programme.desc: Ernie catches up with pro-surfer and artist Otis Carey, and gets a cultural tour in Coffs Harbour with the Gumbaynggirr mob, Rae learns how our cuppas go from crop-to-cup at a local coffee farm.

programme.title: A New ANZAC
programme.desc: The construction of the new Southern Entrance is well underway. The team works day and night to install Super-T beams for the column-less exhibition space of Lower Anzac Hall. The architectural marvels of the Oculus, the spiral timber staircase, and its above eucalyptus-inspired glasswork 'Quiet Skies' come to fruition.

programme.title: Orient Express: A Golden Era of Travel
programme.desc: In the Golden Age of Rail, the Orient Express ran regularly between Paris and Istanbul. Now we retrace its 2,000 mile journey. Heading east, it stops at Germany's 'City of Gold', where most of its jewellery is made. Then on to Munich, a city haunted by its dark past. Finally, it reaches Salzburg, a city that pays tribute to its famous musical family in a most unexpected way.

programme.title: SBS World News Late
programme.desc: Reports and analysis of major national and international news of the day from Australia's only world news service.

programme.title: The King
programme.desc: Did Lackovic commit suicide or was he killed to avenge the murder of Iaccarino? Bruno discovers that his ally had converted to Islam and that a mystery is lurking in the Muslim community at San Michele. Laura, meanwhile, is relentless and questions Bruno's faithful allies, the praetorians.

programme.title: Babylon Berlin
programme.desc: Ullrich examines the murder weapon in the Krempin case. He discovers that it's registered in Bela Gosztony's name. When Ullrich tries to let Rath know this important piece of information, Gennat humiliates him and sends him away.

programme.title: Babylon Berlin
programme.desc: It's the day for which Greta's execution is scheduled. Litten has an idea how he might achieve at least a postponement. He remembers a law, according to which official business, including executions, must cease on days of national mourning.

programme.title: Nordland '99
programme.desc: Lukas, Emma, and Kris are going back to the cabin, where they make an awful discovery. At the same time, Christian reviews the surveillance material from the gas station.

programme.title: Nordland '99
programme.desc: The whole town is in mourning, and the search for Alex takes a new turn. Christian is no longer on the case but makes a promise to an old friend.

programme.title: Mastermind Australia
programme.desc: The legendary quiz that sets the toughest questions on television. Join quizmaster Marc Fennell as each night he puts four contenders to the test.

programme.title: Mastermind Australia
programme.desc: The legendary quiz that sets the toughest questions on television. Join quizmaster Marc Fennell as each night he puts four contenders to the test.

programme.title: Mountain Vets
programme.desc: Vet Patrick gets a surprise while helping a cow through a difficult labour, Maeve gets inventive to save a lamb's sight and Nuala performs delicate surgery on David the hamster.

programme.title: Bamay
programme.desc: A slow TV showcase of the stunning landscapes found in Wiradjuri Country along the waters of the Murrumbidgee River.

programme.title: NHK World English News Morning
programme.desc: English news from Japan's Broadcasting Corporation, NHK, delivering the latest in business, technology, health, environmental and world news.

programme.title: ANC Philippines The World Tonight
programme.desc: Delivers the top stories of the day, covering political, economic, and social sectors on both local and global fronts.

programme.title: DW English News
programme.desc: News and analysis of the top international and European news and current affairs from Berlin, in English.

programme.title: Al Jazeera News
programme.desc: News via satellite from Al Jazeera Satellite Network, Qatar, in English.

programme.title: BBC 6 O'Clock News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news stories from the BBC News team from London, UK, in English.

programme.title: France 24 English News
programme.desc: International news and current affairs television from Paris, offering a French perspective on world events, in English.

programme.title: DD India News Hour
programme.desc: The most recent political, sports, entertainment and socio-economic updates across India and the world from New Delhi, in English.

programme.title: The Cook Up with Adam Liaw Bitesize
programme.sub-title: Lamb Skewers With Tomato Bush Tahini
programme.desc: Join host Adam Liaw and all your favourite chefs, cooks and celebrities for the best bits from The Cook Up kitchen.

programme.title: The Bee Whisperer
programme.desc: Going behind the scenes with migratory beekeeping operations, Charlie gets a once-in-a-lifetime look at the largest insect migration in the world. The he travels to Prime Bees in College Station, Texas to learn operating a forklift for commercial beekeeping.

programme.title: Vintage Voltage
programme.desc: The team set out on a first-time conversion to make a fine handling sports car even better for owner Lee who wants his Porsche to be faster, smoother, and easier to pilot along the B roads.

programme.title: Oman from Above
programme.desc: Oman, strategically located on the Eastern shore of the Arabian Peninsula, is the oldest independent state in the Arab world and home to over 5 million people. A country filled with amazing landscapes, from rugged coastlines, wind-swept dunes, towering mountain ranges, terraced farms clinging to limestone cliffs and waterfalls that plunge to the sea through green fields.

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: The latest national and international news stories from the BBC News team from London, UK, in English.

programme.title: France 24 International News
programme.desc: International news and current affairs television from Paris, offering a French perspective on world events, in English.

programme.title: PBS NewsHour
programme.desc: Reviews and analysis of the day's news presented by Amna Nawaz and Geoff Bennett for the Public Broadcasting Service (PBS) in the United States.

programme.title: SailGP Highlights
programme.desc: Sail GP heads to Guanabara Bay in Brazil for the first time, for Round 4 of the 2026 Championship.

programme.title: Stories From The Cities
programme.desc: Stories from the Cities journeys across Canada, Mexico, and the United States, exploring the host cities of the FIFA World Cup 2026. In this episode, we discover the footballing spirit of Toronto through its people, neighbourhoods, culture, and the everyday moments that define the city.

programme.title: ISU Figure Skating Highlights
programme.desc: All the best moments from the 2026 ISU Figure Skating Synchronised Championships from Salzburg, Austria.

programme.title: Plat du Tour
programme.desc: As the Tour climbs towards the edge of Basque Country, Guillaume cooks a breakfast dish that would fill the belly of any hungry mountain rider. The region is a cultural melting pot and Guillaume celebrates with his version of a Basque-style Shakshuka. Blending Spanish and Arabic flavours with Basque spices, Guillaume's twist on this classic dish is the perfect way to start the day, no matter what part of the world you are in.

programme.title: Surviving WW2
programme.desc: Five civilians recount their WW2 survival. At 11, Simon Gronowski was pushed from a train bound for Auschwitz by his mother, who perished. Toke Yoshihashi, interned for his Japanese heritage, later fought for America in Africa and Italy. Irene Weiss survived Auschwitz after being separated from her family. Mary Lock witnessed the Coventry Blitz, and John Fieldsend was rescued by the Kindertransport, never seeing his parents again.

programme.title: SBS World News
programme.desc: The latest reports and analysis of major national and international news of the day from Australia's only world news service.

programme.title: Jewels of the Mediterranean
programme.desc: On this pocket-sized peninsula, Gibraltar, we go behind the scenes of a wedding at the Sunborn Hotel, it's monkey business for the famous Barbary Macaques and we dive into the challenge of growing the local octopus population.

programme.title: Magical Train Journeys in Switzerland
programme.sub-title: With The Glacier Express from Zermatt to St Moritz
programme.desc: The Glacier Express is called the \.

programme.title: Jersey and Guernsey
programme.desc: St Helier's Port operations manager, Robin, is preparing to welcome an 18th century Swedish replica sailing ship, The Gotheberg to Jersey's shores. But guiding the world's largest wooden sailing ship through the harbour's rocky entrance is a huge undertaking and with bad weather thrown into the mix, Robin and his team need to rise to the challenge and ensure her safe arrival.

programme.title: Finding Your Roots
programme.sub-title: Puritans And Pioneers: Ted Danson, Mary Steenburgen, William H. Macy
programme.desc: Three mainstays of modern-day Hollywood discover family legacies that predate the United States itself. Ted Danson learns of a Connecticut Revolutionary colonel who allowed his slave to buy his freedom. William H. Macy discovers his own family's nonconformist - a Puritan punished for helping Quakers. Mary Steenburgen's ancestors, too, refused to follow the script, including a rowdy drunkard who served under George Washington in the French and Indian War.

programme.title: Snowpiercer
programme.desc: Mr. Wilford (Sean Bean) has arrived. And as this new threat rattles Snowpiercer to its core, Melanie (Jennifer Connelly) makes a move that can't be undone.

programme.title: Snowpiercer
programme.desc: An exchange is made between the two trains, but a far greater revelation might be just over the horizon.

programme.title: Snowpiercer
programme.desc: Melanie (Jennifer Connelly) embarks on her most dangerous mission yet, while Layton (Daveed Diggs) reckons with his personal choices.

programme.title: Matched
programme.sub-title: Johnny And Baljinder's Sikh Wedding / Wasim's Wait For A Bride
programme.desc: Johnny and Baljinder are two very Kiwi Indians who have chosen to have a Sikh arranged marriage, so which aspects of the age-old practice will they adhere to and which do they believe are outdated? And Wasim wants to find a bride, but in a culture where age is everything, has he left it too late to find his Miss Right?

programme.title: Mastermind Australia
programme.desc: The legendary quiz that sets the toughest questions on television. Join quizmaster Marc Fennell as each night he puts four contenders to the test.

programme.title: Mastermind Australia
programme.desc: Mastermind is the legendary quiz that sets the toughest questions on television. Join Mastermind quizmaster Marc Fennell, as each night he puts four contenders to the test. In this round, the specialty subjects are Human Organ Systems, Peep Show: Season 1-3, The Tour De France: 1980 - 1989, and The Wizard Of Oz.

programme.title: Undermining Nazis: Paris' Secret Tunnels
programme.desc: On June 14th, 1940, Paris falls under German occupation. Two doctors, Jean Talairach and Rene Suttel, discover an entrance to the mines of the city. They start a mapping operation of the catacombs in order to help the Resistance and de Gaulle. This human story is partly narrated by Talairach himself, in a moving posthumous testimony, and by Rene Suttel's daughter. It features gripping reenactments, historic archives, and unpublished maps drawn by hand.

programme.title: Destination Flavour China Bitesize
programme.desc: All the best moments and bits from Destination Flavour China, led by famed chef and author Adam Liaw.

programme.title: Destination Flavour China Bitesize
programme.desc: All the best moments and bits from Destination Flavour China, led by famed chef and author Adam Liaw.
